citations.section: The Sign of the Broken Sword
citations.quote: By his right side lay a sword, of which the tip was broken off

nodes.description: General St. Clare's sword with its tip broken off, found lying beside his recumbent bronze memorial statue with the broken point symbolising his disgrace in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: State Document (Blue Envelope)

citations.section: The Adventure of the Second Stain
citations.quote: a letter from a foreign potentate

nodes.description: A letter from a foreign potentate in a blue envelope, the stolen state document whose recovery prevents an international crisis in The Adventure of the Second Stain (The Return of Sherlock Holmes).

nodes.label: Steam Launch Aurora

attributes.description: black with two red streaks, white-banded funnel
attributes.significance: escape vessel for Small and Tonga

citations.quote: The Aurora, sir. She is as trim a little thing as any on the river
the Aurora thundered on, and still we followed close upon her track

nodes.description: The steam launch Aurora, the swift river vessel commanded by Jonathan Small in The Sign of the Four, used for the escape down the Thames until captured by Holmes and Watson on the police pinnace.

nodes.label: steel inner cabin / safe (Prince Otto's room)

citations.section: The Fairy Tale of Father Brown
citations.quote: in this he erected another sort of central cabin or cupboard, lined with steel, like a safe or a battleship

nodes.description: Prince Otto's steel-lined inner cabin or cupboard erected within his castle room, giving him a fortified private study like a safe or a battleship interior in The Wisdom of Father Brown.
normalized_terms: steel inner cabin / safe (prince otto's room)

nodes.label: The Steel Handcuffs

citations.quote: There was a metallic click, and a pair of steel handcuffs had been fastened upon his wrists.

nodes.description: The steel handcuffs that click onto Jefferson Hope's wrists when Holmes and Watson trap him in 221B Baker Street, ending A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.label: stolen silver (fish-knives, forks, cruet-stand, cream-jug)

citations.quote: All the silver gone! Fish-knives and forks gone. Old cruet-stand gone. Even the old silver cream-jug gone.

nodes.description: The stolen silver comprising fish-knives, forks, old cruet-stand, and silver cream-jug, hidden in the dustbin as a blind for Major Putnam's poison plot in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Suit of armour (fourteenth-century tilting armour)

citations.section: The Worst Crime in the World
citations.quote: A complete suit of fourteenth-century armour stood like a sentinel beside the large fireplace.

nodes.description: A complete suit of fourteenth-century tilting armour standing like a sentinel beside the large fireplace at Musgrave Castle, used to conceal the baronet's body in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: sundial (where Champion died)

citations.section: The Strange Crime of John Boulnois
citations.quote: one of those small picturesque surprises common in the old landscape gardening; a kind of small round hill or dome of grass... a sundial in the highest point in the centre

nodes.description: The small round hill or dome of a sundial at Pendragon Park, the spot where Sir Claude Champion died by his own hand in The Strange Crime of John Boulnois (The Wisdom of Father Brown).

nodes.label: Screen (in School for Scandal)

citations.quote: in the principal scene of The School for Scandal one of the principal performers remains for a considerable time on the stage, but is not seen. She is technically 'on,' but she might practically be very much 'off.' That is the Screen of Lady Teazle and the Alibi of Mrs. Mandeville.

nodes.description: The screen behind which a principal performer in The School for Scandal remains hidden on stage for a considerable time, an analogy used in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Telephone

citations.quote: At the other end of it could be seen the telephone which the servant had used to summon the priest.

nodes.description: A telephone installed in Father Brown's study, used by a servant to summon the priest in a story from The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Temple of the Monkey (idol / Monkey's Tail)

citations.quote: I came to a flight of steps, and then to a blind door... I came out again upon gloom... Just in front of me was something that looked like a mountain. I confess I nearly fell on the great stone platform on which I had emerged, to realize that it was an idol.

nodes.description: A sinister idol in an Indian temple, encountered by a character who descends steps into darkness and glimpses it in the shadows, in The Wisdom of Father Brown.
normalized_terms: temple of the monkey (idol / monkey's tail)

nodes.label: The Hound

citations.quote: we could hear the dull rumble of the hound—a baying sound, deep and full of menace.

A hound it was, an enormous coal-black hound, but not such a hound as mortal eyes have ever seen. Fire burst from its open mouth, its eyes glowed with a smouldering glare, its muzzle and hackles and dewlap were outlined in flickering flame.
As to the hound, it was eventually found in the tin mine in the heart of the Grimpen Mire. The phosphorus painting was clear enough on the bones.
As it bounded past the companions, they saw something white, shapeless, a great thing, apparently a large dog.

nodes.description: The Hound is Stapleton's enormous coal-black dog in The Hound of the Baskervilles, daubed with phosphorus to create a supernatural appearance and trained to hunt along a scent, ultimately shot dead by Holmes and Watson on the moor.

nodes.label: The Red Thumb Mark (forged fingerprint)

citations.quote: there were two drops of blood on the bottom of the safe and one or two bloody smears on a piece of paper, and, in addition, a remarkably clear imprint of a thumb

citations.section: Chapter XVI: Thorndyke Plays His Card
citations.quote: the red thumb-mark is a photo-mechanical reproduction of the 'Thumbograph' print — Anstey's closing, summarising Thorndyke's case

nodes.description: A forged fingerprint — a photo-mechanical reproduction of a Thumbograph print — planted as false evidence at the scene of a safe robbery, central to the plot of Dr Thorndyke's The Red Thumb Mark.

nodes.label: Thorndyke's Green Portable Laboratory Case

citations.section: The Case of Oscar Brodski Part II
citations.quote: a small square case covered with Willesden canvas... only a foot square by four inches deep — it contained a fairly complete outfit for a preliminary investigation

nodes.description: Dr Thorndyke's compact portable laboratory, a one-foot-square Willesden-canvas case containing a full set of forensic instruments, carried to crime scenes in The Adventures of Dr Thorndyke.

nodes.label: Threatening letters (red ink on stamp paper)

citations.quote: the red ink on it actually was not dry, and the message ran, 'If you have been to see her today, I shall kill you.'

nodes.description: Anonymous threatening letters written in red ink on stamp paper, sent to a character warning of murder if he visits a woman, appearing in The Innocence of Father Brown.
normalized_terms: threatening letters (red ink on stamp paper)

nodes.label: Three Kings wooden images

citations.quote: He was introduced to the big wooden images of the Three Kings, of painted and gilded wood, who bring the gifts to the children at Christmas.

nodes.description: Three large painted and gilded wooden figures of the Three Kings (the Biblical Magi bearing gifts), displayed as props in a theatrical setting in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: The Thumbograph

citations.quote: Mrs. Hornby, the wife of John Hornby, purchased one of these toys — a Thumbograph — consisting of a small book of blank paper for collecting the thumb-prints of one's friends
he cut the thread and drew from its wrappings a little book bound in red cloth, with the word 'Thumbograph' stamped upon the cover

nodes.description: A small red-cloth-bound novelty book of blank pages used to collect thumb-prints, purchased by Mrs Hornby and later exploited as the source of the forged fingerprint evidence in The Red Thumb Mark.

nodes.label: Toby

citations.quote: I would rather have Toby's help than that of the whole detective force
Toby proved to be an ugly, long-haired, lop-eared creature

nodes.description: Toby is the ugly long-haired lop-eared tracking dog owned by Sherman in The Sign of the Four, borrowed by Holmes to follow the creosote trail from Pondicherry Lodge; Holmes rates Toby's help above the whole detective force.

nodes.label: Trap-door (stage)

citations.quote: this stage was built and used for pantomimes; it would naturally have trap-doors and trick exits of that sort.

nodes.description: A trap-door built into a theatre stage originally constructed for pantomimes, used as a secret exit in a plot in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: book of old travels (Voodoo reference)

citations.quote: He had taken out of his pocket a small, stout, leather-bound volume... 'The only form in which Voodoo--'

nodes.description: A small, stout, leather-bound volume on old travels that contains a reference to Voodoo, produced from Father Brown's pocket during an investigation in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: three-cornered cicatrice / scar (on Green's bald head)

citations.quote: He stared with an almost comical seriousness at his bald forehead, and pointed to a three-cornered cicatrice, long healed, but still discernible. 'Mr Green, I think.'

nodes.description: A long-healed three-cornered cicatrice on a character's bald head, pointed out as a distinguishing physical mark in The Wisdom of Father Brown.
normalized_terms: three-cornered cicatrice / scar (on green's bald head)

nodes.label: Trichinopoli cigars

citations.quote: I asked Putnam if I could get some Trichinopoli cigars

nodes.description: A strong Indian cigar variety sought by a character in an Indian city, whose attempt to purchase them leads to the inciting incident involving the temple monkey in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Hall of weapons (Valentin's armoury)

citations.quote: a hall hung with weapons

nodes.description: A hall hung with weapons in the Paris house of Valentin, the head of the Paris police, serving as the setting for a murder in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Vaudrey's white hat

citations.quote: wearing on his grey head the white hat which he so boldly affected

nodes.description: A boldly worn white hat on the grey head of Sir Arthur Vaudrey, used as a visual identifying detail in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Velasquez Infanta (painting)

citations.quote: the priceless Velasquez Infanta

nodes.description: A priceless Velasquez portrait of the Infanta, the target of a disputed legal ownership case and eventual Raffles theft in Nine Points of the Law (The Amateur Cracksman).

nodes.label: Voodoo Fetish / Rituals

citations.quote: a pan of blood and a charred bone — the voodoo fetish

nodes.description: A voodoo fetish — a pan of blood and a charred bone — found at Wisteria Lodge, evidence of secret South American religious rituals practised by the cook in The Adventure of Wisteria Lodge.

nodes.label: Card with V.V. 341

citations.quote: Vermissa Valley Lodge 341

nodes.description: The Card with V.V. 341 is the cryptic card found at the Birlstone crime scene in The Valley of Fear, decoded by Holmes as standing for Vermissa Valley Lodge 341 of the Scowrers.

nodes.label: wagonette / coach

citations.quote: The coach was a kind of commodious wagonette, invented by the modernist talent of the courier

nodes.description: A commodious wagonette-style carriage devised by a modernist courier to transport a party of travellers in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: prison warder's missing gun

citations.quote: The unfortunate officer had... been hurled from the high wall, his brains beaten out as with a club, and his gun was missing.

nodes.description: A prison warder's missing gun, taken after the warder was hurled from a high wall and battered to death, a key clue in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Warning Letter Sent to Sir Henry

citations.quote: He laid an envelope upon the table, and we all bent over it. It was of common quality, greyish in colour. The address, 'Sir Henry Baskerville, Northumberland Hotel,' was printed in rough characters

nodes.description: An anonymous letter of common greyish quality addressed to Sir Henry Baskerville at the Northumberland Hotel, warning him away from the moor in The Hound of the Baskervilles.
normalized_terms: warning letter sent to sir henry

nodes.label: Watson's Revolver

citations.quote: If our visitor attempts violence, Watson, have your revolver ready.

nodes.description: Watson's revolver, carried at Holmes's request when danger threatens and a symbol of Watson's role as Holmes's armed protector, mentioned from A Study in Scarlet onwards.

nodes.label: Wax candles (without candlesticks)

citations.quote: The wax candles, which have to be stuck in bottle necks because there is nothing else to stick them in.

nodes.description: Wax candles stuck in bottle necks for lack of candlesticks, noted as a detail of poverty or improvisation in a location described in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Wax Dummy of Holmes

citations.quote: a wax figure which I keep for purposes of deception

nodes.description: A wax bust of Holmes placed in the Baker Street window to deceive watchers and assassins, used to protect him and trap Count Sylvius in The Adventure of the Mazarin Stone.

nodes.label: Whitaker's Almanac

citations.quote: Whitaker's Almanac

nodes.description: Whitaker's Almanac, the book used as the key to a cipher message sent to Holmes warning of a plot against John Douglas in The Valley of Fear.

nodes.label: wine glasses

citations.quote: Two wine glasses stood ready for wine on a side-table, but a third lay smashed in a star of crystal upon the carpet.

nodes.description: Two wine glasses set out on a side-table and a third smashed on the carpet in a star of crystal, clues at a crime scene in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Red wine / wine goblet

citations.quote: Flambeau came forward and filled a great goblet with Spanish wine and set it before his friend.

nodes.description: A great goblet filled with Spanish wine by Flambeau and set before Father Brown during a storytelling session in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Wineglass (red wine)

citations.quote: said Father Brown, putting down the wineglass.

nodes.description: A wineglass set down by Father Brown at a significant moment during a conversation in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Wooden Leg (Jonathan Small)

attributes.significance: key clue linking Small to the crime

citations.quote: it is the impression of a wooden stump. The boot-mark and the timber-toe
A crocodile took me and nipped off my right leg above the knee

nodes.description: The Wooden Leg (Jonathan Small) is the timber stump whose impression Holmes reads alongside the boot-mark at Pondicherry Lodge in The Sign of the Four, identifying Small as a one-legged man who lost his limb to a crocodile in India.

nodes.label: Yellow gloves (Vaudrey's)

citations.quote: swinging his walking-stick and flapping his yellow gloves

nodes.description: Yellow gloves worn and flapped by Sir Arthur Vaudrey as a dandy affectation while walking with his walking-stick in The Secret of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Arnhold brothers (guerrilla patriots)

citations.section: The Fairy Tale of Father Brown
citations.quote: He was defeated in several battles by the celebrated Arnhold brothers--the three guerrilla patriots to whom Swinburne wrote a poem

nodes.description: The three Arnhold brothers, celebrated guerrilla patriots to whom Swinburne wrote a poem, who defeated an enemy force in several battles in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Baker Street Irregulars

citations.quote: It's the Baker Street division of the detective police force.

nodes.description: The Baker Street Irregulars are the street-boy detective network led by Wiggins and organised by Holmes in A Study in Scarlet, described as the Baker Street division of the detective police force.

nodes.label: Berkshires Regiment

citations.quote: I was removed from my brigade and attached to the Berkshires, with whom I served at the fatal battle of Maiwand.

nodes.description: The Berkshire Regiment, to which Watson was attached as medical officer and with whom he fought at the battle of Maiwand in A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.label: Brickfall and Amberley (Art Materials)

citations.quote: junior partner of Brickfall and Amberley, who are manufacturers of artistic materials

nodes.description: Brickfall and Amberley, an art materials manufacturing firm of which Josiah Amberley was junior partner before retirement in The Adventure of the Retired Colourman.

nodes.label: Carbonari

citations.section: The Adventure of the Red Circle
citations.quote: the Carbonari, the Italian secret society

nodes.description: The Carbonari, the Italian revolutionary secret society that is the parent body of the Red Circle encountered by Holmes in The Adventure of the Red Circle.

nodes.label: Charing Cross Hospital

citations.quote: "'C.C.H.' I should guess that to be the Something Hunt...perhaps 'Charing Cross Hospital?'" Holmes said.

nodes.description: Charing Cross Hospital, the institution whose initials on Dr. Mortimer's walking stick prompt Holmes's deduction about its owner in The Hound of the Baskervilles.

nodes.label: New Church of Apollo (sun-worship cult)

citations.quote: He calls himself the New Priest of Apollo, and he worships the sun.

nodes.description: A sun-worship cult whose leader calls himself the New Priest of Apollo, active in London and encountered in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: The Clarion / The New Age (newspapers)

citations.quote: in certain articles in The Clarion or The New Age Sir Leopold had been dealt with austerely

nodes.description: The Clarion and The New Age, radical newspapers in which Sir Leopold had been dealt with harshly in articles, referenced in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Crédit Lyonnais

citations.quote: As a matter of prudence, he had rented a safety-deposit box at the Crédit Lyonnais in which to keep it.

nodes.description: The Credit Lyonnais bank, where Arsene Lupin rented a safety-deposit box to store a stolen item in The Extraordinary Adventures of Arsene Lupin.

nodes.label: The Criminologists' Club

citations.quote: the Criminologists' Club met in secret

nodes.description: The Criminologists' Club is the secret society of wealthy amateur criminologists presided over by the Earl of Thornaby in A Thief in the Night, which Raffles infiltrates and robs in the story of the same name.

nodes.label: The Daily News

citations.quote: The Daily News observed that there was no doubt as to the crime being a political one.

nodes.description: The Daily News, a London newspaper that opined the Drebber murder was a political crime in A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.label: Daily Reformer (newspaper)

citations.quote: MR EDWARD NUTT, the industrious editor of the Daily Reformer, sat at his desk

nodes.description: The Daily Reformer, a newspaper whose industrious editor Edward Nutt appears at his desk as the framing device for a story in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: The Daily Telegraph

citations.quote: The Daily Telegraph remarked that in the history of crime there had seldom been a tragedy which presented stranger features.

nodes.description: The Daily Telegraph, a London newspaper that noted the Drebber murder case was one of the strangest in the history of crime in A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.label: The Danite Band (Avenging Angels)

citations.quote: Not the Inquisition of Seville... had ever a more formidable machinery than that which cast a shadow over the State of Utah.

nodes.description: The Danite Band, also called the Avenging Angels, is the Mormon enforcement organisation in A Study in Scarlet that murders John Ferrier and abducts Lucy, its formidable machinery of secret terror surpassing even the Inquisition of Seville.

nodes.label: Eyres of Exmoor (aristocratic family)

citations.quote: the Eyres of Exmoor; or as the old women call it down here, the Devil's Ear of Eyre

nodes.description: The Eyres of Exmoor, an old aristocratic family associated with a local legend known to country women as the Devil's Ear of Eyre, in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: Fifth Northumberland Fusiliers

citations.quote: I was attached to the Fifth Northumberland Fusiliers as Assistant Surgeon.

nodes.description: The Fifth Northumberland Fusiliers, the regiment with which Watson sailed for India and served as Assistant Surgeon before being wounded in A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.label: French Foreign Legion

citations.quote: Commandant O'Brien, of the French Foreign Legion

nodes.description: The French Foreign Legion, the military unit to which Commandant O'Brien belongs in The Innocence of Father Brown.

nodes.label: French nationalist crowd / mob

citations.quote: The mass of the crowd was Nationalist, and already in threatening uproar

nodes.description: A threatening Nationalist crowd that appears in uproar in a scene set in France in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

nodes.label: French Republican Government

citations.quote: the Republican Government put considerable confidence in him as to various chemical improvements

nodes.description: The French Republican Government, which placed considerable trust in a chemist character for chemical improvements in The Wisdom of Father Brown.

citations.quote: Gelder and Co., sculptors in Stepney

nodes.description: Gelder and Co., a firm of sculptors in Stepney from whom a bust involved in a Sherlock Holmes case was commissioned in The Return of Sherlock Holmes.

citations.quote: He intended to leave for America, sailing by one of the steamers.

nodes.description: A steamship company operating services to America, by one of whose vessels Jefferson Hope intended to flee England in A Study in Scarlet.

nodes.description: The Ku Klux Klan (K.K.K.), the American secret society whose threatening message causes a character to cry out in horror in The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes.

Lodge 341

Captain Marvin of the Mine Police

nodes.label: build-pages.ts
nodes.description: build-pages.ts is the script that assembles the graphify GitHub Pages site, generating HTML entries and the index shell from the graph data.

nodes.label: cname
nodes.description: The CNAME constant in scripts/build-pages.ts reads the CNAME environment variable and, if set, writes a CNAME file to the output directory to configure a custom domain for the GitHub Pages deployment.

nodes.label: escapeHtml()
nodes.description: escapeHtml() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that escapes HTML special characters in a string, used by htmlShell() and mdToHtml() when generating static pages.

nodes.label: graphEntryHtml()
nodes.description: The graphEntryHtml() function in scripts/build-pages.ts generates the HTML string for graph.html, an iframe wrapper that embeds the standalone Graphify Ontology Studio as the graph entry page.

nodes.label: htmlShell()
nodes.description: htmlShell() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that wraps page content in the outer HTML shell structure, used alongside escapeHtml().

nodes.label: indexMdPath
nodes.description: The indexMdPath constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the resolved file path to the wiki's index.md file, used to generate the root index.html of the deployed GitHub Pages site.

nodes.label: inlineFormat()
nodes.description: inlineFormat() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that applies inline text formatting transformations, working together with mdToHtml() for rendering.

nodes.label: mdToHtml()
nodes.description: mdToHtml() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that converts a Markdown string to HTML, working with escapeHtml() and inlineFormat() to render wiki page content.

nodes.label: OUT
nodes.description: The OUT constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the path to the docs/ output directory where all built HTML pages, wiki files, studio assets, and deployment artifacts are written.

nodes.label: pageHrefForTitle()
nodes.description: pageHrefForTitle() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that converts a page title to its corresponding HTML href, working alongside slugifyTitle().

nodes.label: resolveTitle()
nodes.description: The resolveTitle() function in scripts/build-pages.ts resolves a raw wiki link target to a canonical title present in titleSet, normalizing whitespace to underscores, and returns null if no match is found.

nodes.label: ROOT
nodes.description: The ROOT constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the current working directory path, used as the repository root from which all other paths in the script are derived.

nodes.label: slugifyTitle()
nodes.description: slugifyTitle() is a function in scripts/build-pages.ts that converts a page title into a URL-safe slug, used by pageHrefForTitle() for link generation.

nodes.label: SRC_GRAPHIFY
nodes.description: The SRC_GRAPHIFY constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the path to the .graphify/ directory within the repository root, used as the source for wiki markdown files and the studio export.

nodes.label: studioDir
nodes.description: The studioDir constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the path to the .graphify/studio/ directory, which must contain a pre-generated standalone studio index.html before the pages build can run.

nodes.label: titles
nodes.description: The titles array in scripts/build-pages.ts is derived from wikiFiles by stripping .md extensions, providing the list of page title strings used to build wiki HTML and populate titleSet.

nodes.label: titleSet
nodes.description: The titleSet Set in scripts/build-pages.ts is constructed from the titles array and used for O(1) lookup during wiki link resolution to detect whether a linked title corresponds to an existing wiki page.

nodes.label: wikiDir
nodes.description: The wikiDir constant in scripts/build-pages.ts holds the path to the .graphify/wiki/ directory, which is the source folder containing all markdown files that are compiled into wiki HTML pages.

nodes.label: wikiFiles
nodes.description: The wikiFiles array in scripts/build-pages.ts is produced by reading the wiki directory and filtering for .md files, forming the complete list of markdown sources that will be compiled into HTML pages.

nodes.label: check-corpus.ts
nodes.description: The check-corpus.ts script that validates the local corpus against the catalog, using main() and the corpusRoot variable alongside catalog.ts, gutenberg.ts, and index.ts to ensure all expected text files are present.

nodes.label: corpusRoot
nodes.description: The corpusRoot constant in scripts/check-corpus.ts holds the path to the corpus/ directory at the repository root, used as the base for enumerating and validating all downloaded corpus work files.

nodes.label: main()
nodes.description: The main() function in scripts/check-corpus.ts validates the full corpus by asserting the public-domain policy, loading the manifest, and verifying each work's SHA-256 digest and byte count against manifest expectations.

nodes.label: deepen-graphify-semantic.ts
nodes.description: deepen-graphify-semantic.ts is the script that runs the deepening step on graphify's raw semantic output, producing a deepened documentary graph using graphify-deepening.ts.

nodes.label: deepened
nodes.description: The deepened variable in scripts/deepen-graphify-semantic.ts holds the result of calling deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph() on the raw graph, representing the semantically enriched graph written to the output file.

nodes.label: raw
nodes.description: The raw variable in scripts/deepen-graphify-semantic.ts holds the parsed SemanticGraph loaded from the input JSON file before any semantic deepening is applied.

nodes.label: fetch-corpus.ts
nodes.description: The fetch-corpus.ts script that downloads and stores corpus text files from Project Gutenberg, using the fetchText() and main() functions and the corpusRoot variable, coordinating with catalog.ts and gutenberg.ts.

nodes.label: corpusRoot
nodes.description: The corpusRoot constant in scripts/fetch-corpus.ts holds the path to the corpus/ output directory where all fetched and stripped Gutenberg texts and metadata files are written.

nodes.label: fetchText()
nodes.description: fetchText() is a function in scripts/fetch-corpus.ts that fetches the text content of a corpus URL, called by main() in the corpus download script.

nodes.label: main()
nodes.description: main() is the entry-point function in scripts/fetch-corpus.ts that orchestrates downloading the public-domain mystery corpus texts using fetchText().

nodes.label: root
nodes.description: The root constant in scripts/fetch-corpus.ts holds the current working directory, used as the repository root from which the corpusRoot path is derived.

nodes.label: generate-graphify-descriptions.ts
nodes.description: The generate-graphify-descriptions.ts script that builds and writes documentary description records from a semantic graph, using buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex() and graphify-descriptions.ts against a resolved graphHash and index.

nodes.label: graphHash
nodes.description: The graphHash variable in scripts/generate-graphify-descriptions.ts holds the SHA-256 hex digest of the graph.json file, embedded in the description index for cache invalidation when the graph changes.

nodes.label: index
nodes.description: The index variable in scripts/generate-graphify-descriptions.ts holds the WikiDescriptionIndex produced by buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex(), which maps node IDs to their generated wiki descriptions.

nodes.label: semanticGraph
nodes.description: The semanticGraph variable in scripts/generate-graphify-descriptions.ts holds the parsed SemanticGraph loaded from the semantic JSON file, passed to buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex() to generate wiki node descriptions.

nodes.label: The Gloria Scott (convict ship)

citations.quote: the Gloria Scott, which was the convict ship... went down in a gale off Falmouth

nodes.description: The Gloria Scott, a convict ship that went down in a gale off Falmouth, whose story forms Holmes's earliest remembered case in The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes.

nodes.label: catalog.ts
nodes.description: catalog.ts defines the corpus catalog and public-domain policy assertions for the mystery saga authors (Chesterton, Doyle, Freeman) used by corpus-checking and fetching scripts.

nodes.label: assertPublicDomainPolicy()
nodes.description: The assertPublicDomainPolicy() function in src/catalog.ts iterates over a list of Work objects and throws if any author or translator fails the life-plus-70 public-domain safety check for the given current year.

nodes.label: CHESTERTON
nodes.description: The CHESTERTON constant in src/catalog.ts is a Person object representing G. K. Chesterton (1874–1936), used as the author field for all Father Brown works in the WORKS catalog array.

nodes.label: DOYLE
nodes.description: The DOYLE constant in src/catalog.ts is a Person object representing Arthur Conan Doyle (1859–1930), used as the author field for all Sherlock Holmes works in the WORKS catalog array.

nodes.label: FREEMAN
nodes.description: Catalog entry object for R. Austin Freeman, author of the Dr. Thorndyke detective series, recording his biographical metadata and associated works in the corpus.

nodes.label: HORNUNG
nodes.description: Catalog entry object for E.W. Hornung, author of the Raffles gentleman-thief series, recording his biographical metadata and associated works in the corpus.

nodes.label: JEPSON
nodes.description: Catalog entry object for Edgar Jepson, translator of French mystery works into English, recording his role and associated translations in the corpus.

nodes.label: LEBLANC
nodes.description: Catalog entry object for Maurice Leblanc, creator of the Arsène Lupin series, recording his biographical metadata and associated works in the corpus.

nodes.label: listSagas()
nodes.description: Returns the complete ordered list of all sagas defined in the mystery corpus catalog, serving as the primary enumeration entry point for downstream processing.

nodes.label: Person
nodes.description: TypeScript type representing a person (author or translator) in the catalog, capturing fields such as name, dates, and role.

nodes.label: TEIXEIRA
nodes.description: Catalog entry object for Alexander Teixeira de Mattos, English translator of the Arsène Lupin novels, recording his role and associated translations in the corpus.

nodes.label: Work
nodes.description: TypeScript type representing a single published work (book or collection) in the corpus, capturing fields such as title, author, translator, and Gutenberg identifiers.

nodes.label: WorkInput
nodes.description: TypeScript type representing the raw, un-normalized input shape used to declare a work entry before it is processed into the canonical Work form.

nodes.label: WORKS
nodes.description: Exported array listing all corpus works defined in src/catalog.ts, used as the authoritative source of works for build and indexing pipelines.

nodes.label: graphify-deepening.ts
nodes.description: graphify-deepening.ts is the source module responsible for deepening the semantic graph by creating curated nodes, edges, and metadata edges during the graph enrichment process.

nodes.label: createCuratedEdge()
nodes.description: createCuratedEdge() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that creates a manually curated edge in the documentary semantic graph, using sourceRefForLocation() and called by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: createCuratedNode()
nodes.description: createCuratedNode() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that creates a manually curated node in the documentary semantic graph, using sourceRefForLocation() and called by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: createMetadataEdge()
nodes.description: createMetadataEdge() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that creates a metadata edge linking a source file to its metadata node in the documentary semantic graph, used by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: createMetadataNode()
nodes.description: createMetadataNode() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that creates a metadata node representing the source file path in the documentary semantic graph, used by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: CURATED_DETAIL_EDGES
nodes.description: Constant array holding hand-curated edge definitions that are injected into the semantic graph during the deepening pass to enrich relationships not automatically inferred.

nodes.label: CURATED_DETAIL_NODES
nodes.description: Constant array holding hand-curated node definitions that are injected into the semantic graph during the deepening pass to add concepts not automatically extracted.

nodes.label: CuratedDetailEdge
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the required fields (source, target, relation, and optional metadata) for a single hand-curated edge entry in the deepening dataset.

nodes.label: CuratedDetailNode
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the required fields (id, label, type, and optional metadata) for a single hand-curated node entry in the deepening dataset.

nodes.label: deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph()
nodes.description: deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph(), the core function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that enriches a semantic graph with curated nodes and edges using helpers createCuratedNode(), createCuratedEdge(), createMetadataNode(), createMetadataEdge(), and edgeKey().

nodes.label: edgeKey()
nodes.description: edgeKey() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that computes a stable key for an edge in the documentary semantic graph, used by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: metadataPathForSourceFile()
nodes.description: metadataPathForSourceFile() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that computes the stable metadata node path for a corpus source file, referenced by createMetadataEdge(), createMetadataNode(), and metadataRefForSourceFile().

nodes.label: metadataRefForSourceFile()
nodes.description: metadataRefForSourceFile() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that returns the reference string for a corpus source file's metadata node, used by createMetadataEdge() and createMetadataNode().

nodes.label: PART_OF_TYPES
nodes.description: Constant listing the node type strings that are eligible to participate in part_of hierarchical relationships when the semantic deepening pass resolves containment edges.

nodes.label: SemanticCitation
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of a citation object attached to semantic graph nodes, capturing source work reference and passage metadata.

nodes.label: SemanticEdge
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of an edge in the semantic graph, including source node id, target node id, relation type, and optional citation list.

nodes.label: SemanticGraph
nodes.description: SemanticGraph is the TypeScript interface in src/graphify-deepening.ts defining the structure of the full documentary semantic graph, referenced by graphify-deepening.ts, graphify-descriptions.ts, and graphify-descriptions.test.ts.

nodes.label: SemanticNode
nodes.description: SemanticNode is the TypeScript interface in src/graphify-deepening.ts defining the shape of a node in the semantic graph, referenced by both graphify-deepening.ts and graphify-descriptions.ts.

nodes.label: sourceRefForLocation()
nodes.description: sourceRefForLocation() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that builds a source reference string from a source file and location, used by createCuratedEdge() and createCuratedNode().

nodes.label: workFromSourceFile()
nodes.description: workFromSourceFile() is a function in src/graphify-deepening.ts that derives a work node identifier from a corpus source file path, used by deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph().

nodes.label: graphify-descriptions.ts
nodes.description: graphify-descriptions.ts is the source module that implements the node description generation logic, including the documentary description index builder and fallback description type constants.

nodes.label: buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex()
nodes.description: buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex() is the main export function in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that builds the full WikiDescriptionIndex for all nodes in a SemanticGraph, calling createRecord() for each node.

nodes.label: CORE_FALLBACK_DESCRIPTION_TYPES
nodes.description: Constant listing the node type strings for which a fallback auto-generated description must always be produced even when no curated description is present.

nodes.label: createRecord()
nodes.description: createRecord(), a function in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that constructs a single description record for a node, delegating to fallbackDescriptionForNode(), sha256(), and sourceRefsForNode().

nodes.label: DESCRIPTION_BY_ID
nodes.description: Map or Record in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that indexes curated description strings by their node ID, enabling O(1) lookup during description assignment.

nodes.label: fallbackDescriptionForNode()
nodes.description: fallbackDescriptionForNode() is a function in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that generates a generic fallback description for a node when no curated description exists, used by createRecord().

nodes.label: sha256()
nodes.description: sha256() is a utility function in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that computes a SHA-256 hash of a string or buffer, used by createRecord() to generate cache keys.

nodes.label: sourceRefsForNode()
nodes.description: sourceRefsForNode() is a function in src/graphify-descriptions.ts that collects evidence references for a node from its attributes, used by createRecord() when building WikiDescriptionRecord objects.

nodes.label: WikiDescriptionIndex
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of the full description index object loaded from disk, mapping node IDs to their WikiDescriptionRecord entries.

nodes.label: WikiDescriptionRecord
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of a single node description record in the index, capturing the description text and its source or confidence metadata.

nodes.label: gutenberg.ts
nodes.description: The gutenberg.ts module that handles Project Gutenberg text retrieval, providing sha256() and slugify() utilities used by the corpus-fetching infrastructure in fetch-corpus.ts and catalog.ts.

nodes.label: sha256()
nodes.description: Utility function in src/gutenberg.ts that computes the SHA-256 hex digest of a string, used to verify the integrity of downloaded Project Gutenberg corpus files.

nodes.label: slugify()
nodes.description: Utility function in src/gutenberg.ts that transforms a work title into a lowercase, URL-safe slug used to construct deterministic corpus file paths.

nodes.label: stripGutenbergBoilerplate()
nodes.description: Function in src/gutenberg.ts that detects and removes the standard Project Gutenberg header and footer boilerplate from a downloaded plain-text file, leaving only the literary content.

nodes.label: index.ts
nodes.description: The main entry-point module (src/index.ts) that exports the corpus data types CorpusManifest, CorpusManifestWork, LoadedCorpus, and LoadedCorpusWork, and the loadCorpusFromRoot() function used by downstream scripts.

nodes.label: CorpusManifest
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of the corpus manifest JSON file, which lists all works included in a built corpus package.

nodes.label: CorpusManifestWork
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of a single work entry within the corpus manifest JSON, capturing identifiers, paths, and metadata for that work.

nodes.label: loadCorpusFromRoot()
nodes.description: loadCorpusFromRoot() is a function in src/index.ts that loads the full mystery corpus from a root directory path, calling loadManifestFromRoot() to locate corpus entries.

nodes.label: LoadedCorpus
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of a fully loaded corpus object, combining the manifest metadata with the in-memory text content of all works.

nodes.label: LoadedCorpusWork
nodes.description: TypeScript interface defining the shape of a single loaded work, pairing its manifest metadata with the full text string of the work.

nodes.label: loadManifestFromRoot()
nodes.description: loadManifestFromRoot() is a function in src/index.ts that loads the corpus manifest JSON from a root directory, used by loadCorpusFromRoot() to enumerate available texts.

nodes.label: The Adventure of the Blanched Soldier

citations.quote: THE ADVENTURE OF THE BLANCHED SOLDIER

nodes.description: The Adventure of the Blanched Soldier, a Case-Book story narrated by Holmes in which he investigates why James Dodd's friend Godfrey Emsworth has been hidden away at Tuxbury Old Park, discovering he has pseudo-leprosy.

nodes.description: The Adventure of the Creeping Man, a Case-Book story in which Holmes investigates Professor Presbury of Camford University, who has been secretly injecting himself with a serum derived from langur monkeys, causing animal-like nocturnal behaviour.
normalized_terms: the adventure of the creeping man

nodes.description: The Adventure of the Illustrious Client, a Case-Book story in which Holmes and Watson are engaged by an anonymous client to prevent Violet de Merville from marrying the Austrian murderer Baron Adelbert Gruner, culminating in a vitriol attack on the Baron.

nodes.description: The Adventure of the Lion's Mane, a Case-Book story narrated by the retired Holmes in which the science master Fitzroy McPherson is found dying on a Sussex beach, with Ian Murdoch suspected until a Cyanea capillata jellyfish is identified as the killer.

nodes.label: The Adventure of Shoscombe Old Place

nodes.description: The Adventure of Shoscombe Old Place, a Case-Book story in which Holmes exposes Sir Robert Norberton's concealment of his sister Lady Beatrice Falder's death in order to preserve her identity until his racehorse Shoscombe Prince wins the Derby.

nodes.label: The Adventure of the Sussex Vampire

nodes.label: The Problem of Thor Bridge

nodes.description: The Problem of Thor Bridge, a Case-Book story in which Holmes proves governess Grace Dunbar innocent of murdering Neil Gibson's Brazilian wife Maria, who staged her own death to frame the governess out of jealousy.

nodes.label: The Adventure of the Three Gables

normalized_terms: the adventure of the three garridebs

nodes.label: The Adventure of the Veiled Lodger

nodes.label: build-pages.test.ts
nodes.description: build-pages.test.ts is the test file for the build-pages script in the mystery pack, exercising runBuildPages() with temp project directories to verify HTML page generation.

nodes.label: runBuildPages()
nodes.description: Test helper function in tests/build-pages.test.ts that invokes the build-pages script against a temporary project directory to perform integration-level validation of the page-build pipeline.

nodes.label: tempDirs
nodes.description: Variable in tests/build-pages.test.ts that holds the set of temporary directories created for the build-pages integration test, used for setup and cleanup.

nodes.label: tempProject()
nodes.description: Variable in tests/build-pages.test.ts holding the temporary project structure (files and directories) assembled to serve as the input for the build-pages integration test.

nodes.label: catalog.test.ts
nodes.description: catalog.test.ts is the test file for the catalog module in the mystery pack, exercising catalog functions including allNames against the corpus entries.

nodes.label: allNames
nodes.description: Test case in tests/catalog.test.ts that calls allNames() and asserts it correctly enumerates every author and translator name registered in the catalog.

nodes.label: graphify-deepening.test.ts
nodes.description: graphify-deepening.test.ts is the test file for the graphify-deepening module in the mystery pack, testing the deepening step against the corpus index.ts and graph output.

nodes.label: graph
nodes.description: Test graph variable in tests/graphify-deepening.test.ts that provides the in-memory graph fixture passed to deepenDocumentarySemanticGraph() during unit tests.

nodes.label: graphify-descriptions.test.ts
nodes.description: The test suite for the graphify descriptions module (tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts), covering buildDocumentaryDescriptionIndex() and related functionality in graphify-descriptions.ts against a SemanticGraph fixture.

nodes.label: graph
nodes.description: Test graph variable in tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts that holds the in-memory graph fixture used to exercise the description-generation functions.

nodes.label: graphPath
nodes.description: Test variable in tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts holding the filesystem path to the JSON file from which the test graph is loaded for description tests.

nodes.label: index
nodes.description: Test variable in tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts holding the WikiDescriptionIndex instance loaded from disk and exercised by the description-generation test suite.

nodes.label: missing
nodes.description: Test case in tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts that verifies the description-generation logic behaves correctly (e.g., uses fallback) when a node has no entry in the description index.

nodes.label: requiredKinds
nodes.description: Test variable in tests/graphify-descriptions.test.ts enumerating the node kind strings that must have descriptions present, used to assert completeness of the description index.

nodes.label: gutenberg.test.ts
nodes.description: gutenberg.test.ts is the test file for the gutenberg module in the mystery pack, testing functions that fetch and process raw Project Gutenberg text files.

nodes.label: raw
nodes.description: Test variable in tests/gutenberg.test.ts holding a raw Project Gutenberg text string (with header and footer boilerplate) used as input to verify stripGutenbergBoilerplate().

nodes.label: load-corpus.test.ts
nodes.description: load-corpus.test.ts is the test file for corpus loading in the mystery pack, exercising loadCorpusFromRoot() against the corpusRoot path in a temporary working directory.

nodes.label: corpusRoot
nodes.description: Test variable in tests/load-corpus.test.ts holding the filesystem path to the test corpus root directory used as the base for corpus-loading integration tests.

nodes.label: workDir
nodes.description: workDir is the test variable in tests/load-corpus.test.ts that holds the temporary work directory path used to exercise loadCorpusFromRoot() during the test suite.

nodes.description: A Thief in the Night, E.W. Hornung's third Raffles story collection featuring A.J. Raffles and Bunny Manders in adventures including A Bad Night, A Trap to Catch a Cracksman, and The Criminologists' Club.

nodes.label: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes

citations.quote: ARSÈNE LUPIN By EDGAR JEPSON AND MAURICE LEBLANC

nodes.label: The Decameron (Boccaccio)

nodes.label: The Book of Life (Holmes's monograph)

citations.quote: From a drop of water a logician could infer the possibility of an Atlantic or a Niagara without having seen or heard of one.

nodes.label: The Case-Book of Sherlock Holmes

nodes.label: De Jure inter Gentes

nodes.label: Gaboriau's Lecoq Novels

nodes.label: The Innocence of Father Brown

citations.quote: Title: The Innocence of Father Brown (first published 1911)

nodes.description: The Innocence of Father Brown (1911), Chesterton's first Father Brown collection, introduces the priest-detective through twelve stories including The Blue Cross, The Invisible Man, and The Hammer of God.

nodes.label: The Red Thumb Mark

citations.quote: THE RED THUMB MARK BY R. AUSTIN FREEMAN

nodes.description: The Red Thumb Mark, R. Austin Freeman's first Dr. Thorndyke novel, centres on a disputed fingerprint and a diamond theft that Thorndyke investigates using scientific forensic methods.

nodes.label: The Return of Sherlock Holmes

citations.quote: The Return of Sherlock Holmes

nodes.description: The Return of Sherlock Holmes, the third Doyle short-story collection, opens with Holmes's resurrection after Reichenbach and gathers thirteen adventures including the Dancing Men, Charles Augustus Milverton, and the Golden Pince-Nez.

nodes.label: The Secret of Father Brown

citations.quote: Title: The Secret of Father Brown (first published 1927)

nodes.description: The Secret of Father Brown (1927), Chesterton's fourth Father Brown collection, gathers eight stories including The Chief Mourner of Marne, The Mirror of the Magistrate, and the frame story revealing Father Brown's method.

nodes.label: The Valley of Fear

citations.quote: The Valley of Fear

nodes.description: The Valley of Fear, a Sherlock Holmes novel by Doyle, pairs a locked-room murder at Birlstone Manor with a flashback to a violent Pennsylvanian coal-mining lodge terrorised by the Scowrers.

nodes.label: The Vanishing Man (The Eye of Osiris)

citations.quote: my friend John Thorndyke, the well-known medico-legal expert, proposed to come down and stay with me

nodes.description: The Vanishing Man (also known as The Eye of Osiris), R. Austin Freeman's novel in which Dr. Thorndyke assists the narrator Dr. Paul Berkeley in a case involving a missing Egyptologist whose will and identity are disputed.
normalized_terms: the vanishing man (the eye of osiris)

nodes.label: The Wisdom of Father Brown

citations.quote: Title: The Wisdom of Father Brown (first published 1914)

nodes.description: The Wisdom of Father Brown (1914), Chesterton's second Father Brown collection, presents twelve more cases including The Absence of Mr Glass, The God of the Gongs, and The Fairy Tale of Father Brown.

nodes.label: The Hound of the Baskervilles

citations.quote: The Hound of the Baskervilles

nodes.description: The Hound of the Baskervilles, a Sherlock Holmes novel by Doyle, investigates the legend of a supernatural hound menacing the Baskerville family on Dartmoor, with Stapleton unmasked as the culprit.

nodes.label: The Sign of the Four

citations.quote: The Sign of the Four

nodes.description: The Sign of the Four, Doyle's second Holmes novel, follows Holmes and Watson as they trace a stolen Agra treasure through a network of intrigue involving a one-legged man and a pygmy archer on the Thames.

nodes.label: Henri Murger's Vie de Bohème

citations.quote: He took a volume from his shelf... reading from a book of light literature.

nodes.description: La Vie de Bohème is the work mentioned in the Arsène Lupin stories.
